sn1.21:1.2: As she was standing there, the devatā recited this verse in the Blessed One’s presence:
sn1.21:2.2: as if his head were on fire,
sn1.21:2.3: a monk should live the wandering life —mindful—
sn1.21:2.4: for the abandoning of sensual passion.”
sn1.21:3.1: The Buddha: “As if struck by a sword,
sn1.21:3.2: as if his head were on fire,
sn1.21:3.3: a monk should live the wandering life —mindful—
